description: Eldritch horror, fate, inevitability, the void, and world-ending threat.
traits: Soul Fragmenter
It splits itself into lesser copies, each with its own mind and motives. The fragments bicker and scheme, and destroying one only makes the others stronger.
Atmospheric Presence
It carries its own alien atmosphere. The air changes before it arrives, a warning written in strange pressure and wrong temperature.
Void Touched
Part of it exists in the emptiness between stars. Attacks pass through the gaps, and its wounds reveal nothing inside, just cold, endless void.
Inevitability Engine
What it declares will happen, happens. Prevention is futile. The only hope is finding a loophole in the wording before the inevitable arrives.
Catastrophe Herald
It appears before disasters, not as their cause but as their witness. It is always blamed and never believed when it tries to warn.
Unmaker's Touch
Its touch unravels things backward through their history until they never existed at all. No remains, no evidence, not even a memory left behind.
Erasure Wake
What it touches begins to disappear, memories first, then physical form. Victims are forgotten by the world before they finish dying.
Apocalypse Seed
It carries a world-ending catastrophe inside it like a pregnancy. Killing it improperly triggers the very destruction everyone fears. It must be contained, not destroyed.
Walking Apocalypse
Reality itself degrades with every step it takes. Cults worship its footsteps, and the world grows thinner in every place it has walked.
Finality Bringer
Near it, death is permanent. No resurrection, no return, no second chance. Even the gods hesitate before its absolute finality.
Void Singer
Its songs create pockets of true nothingness. Not darkness, not emptiness, but the absence of existence itself. Followers seek it out, wanting obliteration.
Existence Tax
It charges a toll for existing in its presence. Memories, years of life, abilities, all are acceptable payment. Its ledger of reality debts is vast.
Horizon Walker
It exists always at the horizon, visible but forever unreachable. Approaching only pushes it further away. An eternal chase with no possible end.
The Narrative End
It appears when stories reach their conclusion, feeding on finales and last words. It collects famous endings and hungers for the next.
Origin Eater
It devours the beginnings of things. Heroes forget why they took up the sword. Villains lose the grievance that set them on their path.
